mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a rank correlation above 0.05 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in greater than 50% of observations during documented recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 195.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above threshold yields positive forward returns in greater than 55% of cases or predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative returns in greater than 60% of instances or loses statistical significance after controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that directly improves corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices falling below 108.0 signal contracting manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, which depresses earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO below 108.0 fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 45% or rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.
